summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Logarithmic transformation'?

"Logarithmic transformation" means: Taking the logarithm of variable values to linearize exponential or power relationships for regression analysis. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Exponential model'?

"Exponential model" means: A model of the form y = ab^x, which becomes linear when expressed as log(y) = log(a) + x·log(b). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Power model'?

"Power model" means: A model of the form y = ax^b, which becomes linear when expressed as log(y) = log(a) + b·log(x). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Linearization'?

"Linearization" means: The process of transforming non-linear data so that a linear regression model is appropriate. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Back-transformation'?

"Back-transformation" means: Converting predictions from transformed scale back to the original scale (e.g., exponentiating a log-predicted value). The other options describe different concepts.
